name: Stephanie Ramsey's Cinnabon Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Milk, just enough to fluff
1 cup Pecans or walnuts, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
BUNS: In a small bowl, combine water, yeast and sugar. Stir until dissolved. Set aside. In a large bowl, make pudding mix according to package directions. Add butter, eggs and salt; mix well. Add the yeast mixture; blend. Gradually add flour and knead until smooth, adding "sprinkles" of flour as needed to control stickiness. Once the dough is no longer sticky and is soft and silky feeling (like baby's behind), the dough is ready. Place in a very large greased bowl. Cover and let rise until double in bulk (about 1 hour). Punch down and let rise again (about 45 minutes). On a lightly floured surface, roll out to a 34-inch x 21-inch rectangle. Spread 1 cup of melted butter over the surface.
CINNABON TOPPING: In a small bowl, mix brown sugar and cinnamon together. Sprinkle all over the top of the surface. Roll up very tightly. With a knife, make a notch every 2 inches. With string or thread, place under the roll by the notch and criss-cross over to cut the roll (makes a nice clean cut). Place on a greased baking pan, 2-inches apart. Lightly press the rolls down with your hands (just a little, it helps to hold them together better). Cover and let rise until double again. Bake at 350 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes. Take them out when they just start to turn golden. Do not over-bake! Frost warm rolls with Cream Cheese Frosting.
CREAM CHEESE FROSTING: Combine all ingredients and mix until smooth. Spread on very warm rolls. Rolls are best when served warm.
CARAMEL STICKY BUNS & TOPPING: In a saucepan or in your microwave oven, combine butter, brown sugar and corn syrup. Heat and stir until blended. Pour topping into the bottom of the pans (pans) you are using for the rolls, place unbaked cinnamon rolls on top of the caramel sauce. Allow to rise in a warm place until double in size (about 1 hour). Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes or until lightly brown. After removing pans from the oven, cover with foil; invert onto cooling racks, cool for 1 minute, and remove pan. Cool before serving.
